lead_profile_text: Existing STL operator. Former home live on Airbnb since March generating ~£2,500/month gross at 60% occupancy (~£1,800 profit/month after bills). Partnership (Kennedy Chadwick Estates) with partner and stepdad. Rossendale Road: £117k purchase, £275k expected valuation, ready in ~2 weeks. Pipeline: third house + land for two further properties.
emotional_profile: Ambitious entrepreneur. Emotional attachment to high-end renovation investment. Frustrated by Booking.com guest quality — active pain point. Wants to scale without becoming operationally burdened. Values professional credibility.
delay_trigger_phrases: Test the long-term rental market first
Test long-let for a month — if no suitable tenant, go STL
delay_timeline: ~2 weeks (property ready) + potential 1-month LTL test period
green_light_trigger_phrases: Risk of becoming manual cleaners instead of entrepreneurs
Two changeovers and we'd need to hire cleaners anyway

top_objections.text: Two simultaneous changeovers = becoming manual cleaners, not entrepreneurs
top_objections.zac_response: Stayful manages all cleaning and coordination — scalability is the core pitch

top_objections.text: Booking.com guest quality — drug preparation, £200 dining table destroyed, no compensation
top_objections.zac_response: Guest screening with deposits and ID checks; AirCover for damage protection

top_objections.text: STL worst-month projection (£952) vs monthly costs (£1,100) — potential small monthly loss
top_objections.zac_response: Worst-month outlier; honest framing applied; annual average significantly above LTL

top_objections.text: £100k renovation exposure — granite countertops throughout, emotional attachment
top_objections.zac_response: £200 deposit, ID checks, AirCover protection layers

top_objections.text: Considering LTL test first (£1,300-1,400/month estate agent estimate)
top_objections.zac_response: Acknowledged; 6-month STL trial mitigates risk with exit option thereafter

top_questions.text: How do you handle damage and guest quality vs Booking.com?
top_questions.answer: Deposits, ID checks, AirCover £100k — all handled by Stayful without owner involvement

top_questions.text: Can Stayful manage multiple properties as we grow?
top_questions.answer: Yes — portfolio partnership model; Nathan's pipeline of 3-4 properties is exactly what Stayful is built for

top_questions.text: What does Slack + reporting look like?
top_questions.answer: Dedicated Slack channel, calendar access, monthly statements 1st-5th, quarterly review calls

what_moved_them: Guest screening directly resolves live Booking.com pain point
Scalability framing — entrepreneurs not manual cleaners
Slack channel and professional management layer
AirCover protection for high-end renovation investment
what_fell_flat: Worst-month projection below monthly costs — no full resolution
LTL alternative remains credible for this specific tight-margin property

conversion_gaps.gap: Tight worst-month margin creates legitimate LTL hesitation
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Send full 12-month projection showing only 1-2 months near breakeven; Year 2 ramp clears £1,100 every month
Follow up on Tracy (mum) call June 2nd re Dugdale Street; position Stayful as portfolio partner

offer_framing: No offer made — standard 15% + VAT discussed
zac_close_phrase: Action plan sent within 15-20 minutes. June 2nd call with Tracy re Dugdale Street — separate pipeline.
zac_income_intro: Rossendale Road: worst month £952, average well above LTL. As you scale to 3-4 properties the management partnership becomes increasingly valuable.
zac_transition_phrases: The bigger opportunity here is what happens when you get to three or four properties
That Booking.com experience is precisely what our vetting process is designed to prevent
zac_reassurance_phrases: £200 deposit, ID checks — we vet every guest before keys are issued
Monthly statements, Slack — you'll always know exactly what's happening at each property
